element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Player begins with free land and limited funds to establish foundational infrastructure
Low-complexity, low-cost attractions available immediately for basic park operation
Revenue generation through concessions; customizable attributes affect visitor behavior
Lack of staff creates cascading problems: messy paths, ride breakdowns, crime, unhappy visitors
Player allocates funding to unlock new rides, improve durability, enhance staff efficiency, expand bus capacity
High-complexity, expensive attractions requiring track layout; unlocked through research
Rides explode if neglected; requires ongoing mechanical staff attention
Themed entertainment to boost visitor satisfaction; platform-dependent availability
Financial strategy: borrowing capital vs. pricing for visitor access
Unhappy visitors attract thugs who vandalize park (pop balloons, steal food, assault entertainers)
Failed negotiations cause staff strikes or shipment loss; adds complexity at higher difficulty levels
Full simulation difficulty only; requires comprehensive resource management
After profitably selling initial park, player relocates to new terrain/economy/weather conditions

typical_relief_triggers: Expanding staff to restore park order
Generating profit threshold to afford new rides
typical_tension_triggers: Ride breakdown/explosion due to lack of maintenance
Running low on cash before loan maturity
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Calendar-based yearly cycles with escalating management complexity; player can adjust game speed; tension builds through resource depletion and staff management failures, relieved by successful research completions and profitable seasons

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Research system gates attraction availability; player can tour park or individual rides to assess layout and visitor flow

difficulty_curve.early_game: Minimal management: build basic rides/shops, hire essential staff, balance entry price with loan repayment
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Staff management complexity peaks; maintenance demands increase; research becomes critical gating mechanism; visitor satisfaction directly impacts park stability
difficulty_curve.late_game: Financial strategy deepens with worldwide relocation; platform-dependent features (some versions require negotiation, stock management, advanced logistics)
notable_spikes: First major ride breakdown when maintenance staff insufficient
Visitor unhappiness triggering organized vandalism waves
Failed negotiation causing unexpected staff strike or supply disruption
Park relocation requiring adaptation to new economy/weather/terrain variables

notable_level_patterns.description: Game offers three difficulty levels (sandbox, medium, full) with player switching allowed at any time. Sandbox removes research, negotiation, stocks, and shares complexity; full simulation requires mastery of all systems including logistics.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Starting location (UK) in sandbox mode vs. full simulation

notable_level_patterns.description: Calendar system judges player performance annually against rivals; creates recurring pressure points and relief moments as yearly awards and trophies are distributed
notable_level_patterns.example_level: End-of-year evaluation triggering cash awards or competitive pressure

notable_level_patterns.description: Player must accumulate sufficient profit from initial park, auction it, then relocate to new worldwide plots with varying economic/weather/terrain conditions that affect gameplay difficulty
notable_level_patterns.example_level: UK (starting) → subsequent worldwide locations with desert/glacier/tropical variations
